title: China-to-Uganda Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: EMC and Power Quality — IEC 62920 / IEC 61000 Evidence for PV Inverters
rows.target_requirement: For Uganda PV inverter projects, EMC evidence should be aligned to IEC 62920 for photovoltaic power conversion equipment and the applicable IEC 61000-series emissions, immunity, harmonic, and flicker tests requested by the utility, project owner, or UNBS/PVoC conformity assessment route. IEC 62920 is the dedicated EMC standard for PV power conversion equipment. Grid-connected systems should also show that EMC and harmonic performance is compatible with Uganda's 50 Hz, 240/415 V network and the connection agreement.
target_standards: IEC 62920 — Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment
IEC 61000 series — Electromagnetic compatibility test methods and limits, including harmonics and flicker where applicable
UNBS PVoC conformity assessment for electrical and electronic goods including solar equipment
Utility or project-owner power-quality requirements
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter EMC evidence commonly relies on GB/T 37408-2021, NB/T 32004, and test reports from CNAS-accredited laboratories. These reports may include EMC and electrical-performance tests but should not be assumed to cover IEC 62920 or the exact IEC 61000 limits and configurations requested for a Ugandan project.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: GB/T EMC reports are not automatically IEC 62920 / IEC 61000 evidence for Uganda. Exporters should map each Chinese EMC test clause to the requested IEC clause, identify missing emissions, immunity, harmonic, or flicker tests, and confirm whether retesting is needed for the model, firmware, power rating, enclosure, cabling, and Uganda voltage configuration.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory when requested by UNBS/PVoC conformity assessment, ERA or utility project review, the connection agreement, or the project owner; otherwise a core technical due-diligence item for PV inverter acceptance.
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese EMC evidence may satisfy Chinese domestic certification or procurement expectations but is not automatically accepted as Uganda IEC evidence.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not submit a China GB/T EMC certificate as a drop-in substitute for IEC 62920 or IEC 61000 evidence. Build a clause map, close missing EMC and harmonic tests, and confirm the required test configuration with UNBS, the utility engineer, or the project owner before shipment.

rows.requirement: ERA and Utility Grid-Connection Review — Anti-Islanding, 50 Hz / 240 V / 415 V Settings, and Commissioning Evidence
rows.target_requirement: Uganda grid-tied solar PV projects should be treated as project-level approvals involving ERA licensing or permit review where applicable, plus connection acceptance by the relevant network operator. ERA publishes licensing procedures and electricity-sector guidelines and standards, including renewable-energy feed-in-tariff and off-grid / isolated-grid instruments. For inverter documentation, the practical technical file should include IEC 62116 anti-islanding, IEC 61727 utility-interface evidence, protection settings, harmonic and power-quality evidence, and commissioning records mapped to Uganda's 50 Hz, 240/415 V supply. Distribution connection is commonly handled by UMEME or UEDCL depending on the service area and current concession arrangements; transmission-scale projects should also check UETCL interface requirements.
target_standards: ERA licensing procedure and electricity-sector regulatory instruments
IEC 62116 — Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters — Test procedure of islanding prevention measures
IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic systems — Characteristics of the utility interface
Uganda low-voltage supply context — 50 Hz, 240 V single-phase, 415 V three-phase
UMEME / UEDCL / UETCL connection and commissioning requirements, as applicable to the project
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-connection evidence commonly includes GB/T 19964-2024, NB/T 32004, GB/T 37408-2021, and State Grid or China Southern Power Grid protection settings. These documents are based on Chinese grid conditions and Chinese approval processes. They may support technical review but do not grant ERA approval, UMEME/UEDCL connection acceptance, UETCL interface acceptance, or a Ugandan commissioning sign-off.
china_standards: GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: China grid reports and firmware settings do not transfer automatically to Uganda. Exporters should prepare a Uganda-specific grid file covering anti-islanding, voltage and frequency trip windows, reconnection delay, ride-through behavior where required, harmonics, protection coordination, settings lockout, and commissioning evidence. Firmware should be parameterised and documented for Uganda's 240/415 V, 50 Hz network rather than left at China 220/380 V defaults.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory as a practical project gate for grid-connected systems to the extent required by ERA licensing, the relevant distribution or transmission utility, the connection agreement, and commissioning procedures.
mandatory_status.cn: Not equivalent — China grid-code evidence supports technical comparison but does not create Ugandan utility permission to connect.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat Uganda grid connection as a separate project gate from China grid approval. Map the inverter's IEC 62116, IEC 61727, protection, harmonics, and commissioning evidence to ERA and the relevant UMEME, UEDCL, or UETCL connection file before energization.

rows.requirement: Uganda Solar Project Context — Hydro-Dominant Grid, C&I Solar, Off-Grid, Mini-Grid, and GET FiT Experience
rows.target_requirement: Uganda's generation mix is strongly shaped by Nile hydro assets such as Bujagali and Karuma, while solar PV has developed through utility, C&I, off-grid, mini-grid, and rural-electrification channels. ERA lists renewable-energy feed-in-tariff and off-grid / isolated-grid guidance among its regulatory instruments. For a Chinese inverter supplier, this means the compliance path is project-specific: an off-grid or mini-grid inverter may face different ERA, UNBS, installer, and commissioning evidence from a grid-exporting C&I or utility-scale inverter.
target_standards: ERA Uganda Renewable Energy Feed-In-Tariff guidelines
ERA off-grid systems guidelines
ERA isolated-grid integration guidelines
Project-specific utility and owner technical specifications
rows.china_equivalent: China distributed PV and utility-scale PV evidence is developed under Chinese policy, grid-operator rules, and domestic standards such as NB/T 32004, GB/T 37408-2021, and GB/T 19964-2024. Those files should be treated as product and engineering inputs only; they do not identify the Ugandan project category or the local approval pathway.
china_standards: Chinese grid operator distributed PV and utility-scale PV procedures
rows.gap: Gap: The first Uganda decision is not only product testing; it is identifying the project pathway. Determine whether the inverter is for import resale, an off-grid system, an isolated mini-grid, C&I self-consumption, net export, or a utility-scale plant, then align the evidence package to UNBS, ERA, the relevant utility, and the project owner.

mandatory_status.target: Project-dependent; ERA, utility, and project-owner requirements differ between off-grid, isolated-grid, C&I, and grid-exporting systems.
mandatory_status.cn: Not equivalent — China project categories and approvals do not classify the Uganda project pathway.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Classify the Uganda solar project before relying on any Chinese inverter certificate. Off-grid, mini-grid, C&I, and grid-exporting projects can trigger different ERA, utility, and owner review gates even when the same inverter model is used.

rows.requirement: UNBS PVoC Import Conformity — Certificate of Conformity for Electrical, Electronic, and Solar Equipment
rows.target_requirement: UNBS operates Uganda's PVoC programme for goods verified by appointed inspection agents in the country of export. UNBS lists electrical and electronics including solar equipment among categories covered under PVoC. A Chinese PV inverter shipment should therefore be screened for PVoC coverage before export and, where covered, should obtain the required Certificate of Conformity through an appointed PVoC agent before import clearance. The PVoC gate is separate from ERA project licensing and utility connection acceptance.
target_standards: UNBS Pre-Export Verification of Conformity (PVoC) programme
UNBS PVoC categories — electrical and electronics including solar equipment
UNBS appointed PVoC agents and inspection / clearance regulations
Applicable Uganda Standards (US), including adopted IEC standards where declared or requested
rows.china_equivalent: China export files commonly include factory test reports, CQC/CGC voluntary certificates, CNAS laboratory reports, CCC-exemption statements where relevant, and GB/T 37408-2021 or NB/T 32004 evidence. These documents can support PVoC assessment but are not themselves a UNBS Certificate of Conformity and do not bypass the appointed-agent PVoC process.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
Chinese voluntary CQC / CGC PV inverter certification and CNAS laboratory reports
rows.gap: Gap: PVoC is a shipment and import-clearance gate, not just a product-standard comparison. Exporters should confirm HS code and product category with UNBS or the appointed PVoC agent, provide model-specific safety, EMC, label, manual, and test evidence, and secure the Certificate of Conformity before shipping goods that fall within PVoC coverage.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for imported goods that fall within UNBS PVoC coverage; Certificate of Conformity is a practical import-clearance gate.
mandatory_status.cn: Not equivalent — Chinese certificates and test reports support assessment but are not UNBS PVoC Certificates of Conformity.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Confirm PVoC coverage before export because UNBS lists electrical and electronic goods including solar equipment under PVoC. A China GB/T or CQC/CGC file can support review but does not replace the UNBS-appointed-agent Certificate of Conformity process.

rows.requirement: IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 Safety Evidence — PV Inverter Electrical Safety
rows.target_requirement: PV inverter safety evidence for Uganda should be prepared around IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2, the IEC safety standards for power converters used in photovoltaic power systems and the inverter-specific requirements. UNBS may adopt IEC standards as Uganda Standards, and project owners, utilities, lenders, EPC contractors, or PVoC agents may request IEC 62109 type-test certificates for the specific inverter model or family. Safety evidence should be model-specific and should cover ratings, environmental limits, protection class, markings, manuals, and installation constraints.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements
IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters
UNBS Uganda Standards adoption and PVoC conformity assessment where applicable
ERA, utility, lender, EPC, and project-owner technical specifications
rows.china_equivalent: China-side evidence commonly includes GB/T 37408-2021, NB/T 32004, factory routine tests, and voluntary Chinese PV inverter certificates. These documents may address safety topics but should not be treated as IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test certificates unless the report explicitly states IEC 62109 compliance for the exact model and configuration.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
Factory routine and outgoing inspection reports
rows.gap: Gap: Chinese domestic safety evidence does not automatically prove IEC 62109-1/-2 compliance for Uganda. Exporters should obtain or map IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test evidence from a competent accredited laboratory, check that the model family and firmware version are covered, and align labels and manuals to the PVoC and project technical file.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where incorporated into Uganda Standards, PVoC assessment, utility/project specifications, lender requirements, or owner procurement documents; strongly expected for bankable grid-tied PV inverter packages.
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese GB/T or voluntary certification evidence is not automatically equivalent to IEC 62109-1/-2 type testing.

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat IEC 62109-1/-2 as the core safety evidence package for Uganda PV inverter review unless the current UNBS, PVoC agent, utility, or project owner specifies a different accepted route. China GB/T 37408-2021 or NB/T 32004 files should be mapped, not assumed equivalent.
